content: Pharmacogenomics studies how genetic variation affects drug response. Key genes: CYP2D6 (opioids, beta-blockers, SSRIs, tamoxifen), CYP2C19 (clopidogrel, PPIs, antidepressants), CYP2C9/VKORC1 (warfarin), TPMT/NUDT15 (thiopurines), UGT1A1 (irinotecan), SLCO1B1 (statin myopathy risk), HLA-B*1502 (carbamazepine Stevens-Johnson syndrome in Asians), HLA-B*5701 (abacavir hypersensitivity). Polymorphisms create metabolizer phenotypes: poor (no function), intermediate (reduced), extensive/normal (wild-type), ultrarapid (gene duplication/amplification). FDA includes pharmacogenomic information in >300 drug labels; CPIC (Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium) provides evidence-based dosing guidelines.
